description:
Goal
Make compiler2 native lowering and codegen consume one shared demand-derived boundary transport model so direct-only callables stay direct across input, call, return, and resume boundaries without allocating or publishing first-class closures.

Problem being fixed
src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs originally materialized every BackendStep::FunctionRef/Lambda with MakeFnRef/MakeClosure, selected callable boundaries by capture types, and scanned all callable-producing steps as identity needs. We have now learned that fixing only producer and call lowering is insufficient. Inputs and captures can already stay direct, but return/resume transport still collapses at DeliveredResume because the current internal ReturnAbi model can express only Value or TupleFields. That narrower model forces some direct-only callables back into ValueRef and reintroduces the very status quo we are trying to remove.

Concrete failing seam
Repro: compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_enum_reduce_through_compiler2_codegen currently fails during LowerNativeProgram because a direct-only zero-capture callable returns through a continuation resume boundary and is rebound as a runtime value. Diagnostic shape: a callee expecting direct-callable transport instead observes Runtime(Var(...)). This proves the unresolved seam is shared boundary transport, not just direct-call lowering.

Code seams
- src/compiler2/jobs/artifact.rs: shared internal boundary transport derivation, especially return/resume transport currently modeled by ReturnAbi.
- src/compiler2/jobs/backend.rs and src/ir_interp/backend.rs: DeliveredResume binding and internal boundary transport must consume the same layout family as executable inputs.
- src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs: lowering of BackendStep::FunctionRef/Lambda, entry_signature, bind_entry_input, entry_capture_args, entry_call_args_from_value, delivered_args_for_abi, and any remaining callable identity / boundary recovery.
- src/compiler2/native_codegen/*: NativeDemandAbi and delivered-shape/codegen entry harnesses must consume the shared transport facts rather than a narrower return-only model.

Required data-model change
Replace the idea that internal returns are a smaller special case than inputs. There must be one shared transport layout family for runtime-carried values at internal boundaries. The exact names can differ, but it must be able to represent at least:
- omitted
- runtime value lane
- tuple-field lanes
- direct-callable captures with exact target identity

That shared layout must be authoritative for:
- executable params
- direct call args
- continuation captures
- executable returns
- delivered-resume inputs

Algorithm
1. Generalize the current input layout idea into a shared runtime value transport layout for internal boundaries.
2. Derive both parameter transport and return/resume transport from settled ExecutableRuntimeDemand. Do not recover return transport from types.
3. Thread that layout through BackendProgram / NativeProgram handoff so DeliveredResume carries the same kind of fact as executable inputs.
4. Lower direct-only callable values as executable target plus demanded captures everywhere this shared layout applies. No MakeClosure/MakeFnRef is emitted for direct-only demand.
5. Lower first-class callable values only where callable materialization demand explicitly requires them.
6. Keep callable-entry ABI separate from executable-internal transport ABI. Callable entries are publication wrappers for first-class callable values only.
7. Keep capture/context placement a deliberate ABI choice. Prefer internal signatures that keep user inputs first and append closure/capture context only where the settled transport layout requires it.

Removal target
- Delete settled_callable_boundary capture-type selection if demand-derived callable boundary ids or materialization facts make it obsolete.
- Delete callable identity collection from all backend steps; identities should come only from demanded first-class entries and any explicit publication wrappers that remain necessary.
- Delete the assumption that ReturnAbi::{Value, TupleFields} is sufficient as the internal return/resume transport model.
- Delete native/backend special casing that reconstructs callable obligations after artifact planning.

Checks
- Native JIT/AOT tests pass for direct lambda call, escaped lambda, opaque callable, enum reducer cases, quicksort, and direct closure-target fixtures.
- Enum.reduce class fixtures prove that direct-only callable values stay direct across call, return, and delivered-resume boundaries.
- --log-telemetry confirms direct-only callable demand does not emit MakeClosure/MakeFnRef or callable_entries.
- No native code path publishes callable boundaries from undemanded lambda/function-ref steps.
- No internal return/resume path collapses a direct-only callable back into ValueRef solely because it crossed a continuation boundary.

comments.text: Current seam after mirroring demand-shaped local values into native executable inputs and entry captures: native direct-only callable transport still collapses at DeliveredResume because ReturnAbi can only express Value or TupleFields. Repro: compiler2_native_program_jit_runs_enum_reduce_through_compiler2_codegen fails in LowerNativeProgram with 'native call arg 7 for callee 66 expected direct-callable 65 with 0 capture(s), observed Some(Runtime(Var(7)))'. This indicates a direct-only callable returning through a continuation resume boundary and being rebound as a runtime value. Next correct step is to generalize internal return/resume transport from ReturnAbi::{Value,TupleFields} to the same value-layout model used for runtime params/captures, then thread that through artifact planning, backend interpreter DeliveredResume binding, native entry signatures, delivered_args_for_abi, and NativeDemandAbi/codegen delivered-shape handling. Do not restore old closure materialization to satisfy the test.

title: Compiler2 native: lower direct callable demand without first-class closure materialization
